meta_title: For Freelancers: Git with AI to Solve overwhelmed by AI tools (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to Git with AI and overcome overwhelmed by AI tools. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: overwhelmed by AI tools

content.seo_title: Git with AI: The Freelancer's Guide to Shipping Faster
content.seo_description: Stop drowning in AI tools. Master Git with AI using Antigravity and Claude Code to streamline your workflow, impress clients, and ship production-ready apps.
content.answer_block: Stop chasing every new AI tool. The secret to professional velocity is integrating AI directly into your version control workflow. By pairing Antigravity for architectural planning and Claude Code for terminal-based execution, you transform your Git repository into an automated engine, ensuring consistent code quality and rapid delivery for every client project.
content.intro:
The modern tech landscape is a graveyard of abandoned AI subscriptions. You spend more time context-switching between browser tabs and LLM prompts than actually shipping features. This fragmentation kills your billable hours and leaves your codebase looking like a patchwork quilt of inconsistent logic.

But what if your Git repository became your lead developer? By anchoring your AI interactions to version control, you create a repeatable, auditable, and professional workflow. It is time to stop being an 'AI user' and start being an 'AI builder' who leverages Git as the single source of truth for your agentic development.

steps.title: Establish the Contextual Foundation
steps.description: Before writing code, define the project architecture within the repo to ensure your AI agents understand the existing directory structure and technical constraints.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the existing directory structure and generate a comprehensive 'Project-Manifest.md' that outlines the tech stack, key dependencies, and coding standards for this repository.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal --read-only --ask 'Review the current codebase and summarize the architecture in a new README.md'

steps.title: Automate Feature Branching
steps.description: Never manually write a commit message or branch description again. Use AI to generate semantic, descriptive branches based on your current task list.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Based on the next item in my backlog, create a descriptive, conventional-commit-compliant branch name and a summary of the required changes.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal --ask 'Create a new branch for the authentication module update and write a summary file explaining the scope'

steps.title: Agentic Code Implementation
steps.description: Execute code changes directly within the terminal, allowing the AI to read your local files and apply patches without manual copy-pasting.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Implement the requested authentication middleware. Ensure it follows existing patterns in /src/middleware and includes proper error handling.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'implement the new JWT auth middleware in src/middleware/auth.ts'

steps.title: Automated Code Review & PR Generation
steps.description: Before pushing, have your AI agent perform a self-code-review to catch edge cases, security flaws, and style violations.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a Senior Staff Engineer. Review the changes in the current branch for security vulnerabilities, performance bottlenecks, and adherence to project standards.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal --ask 'Review the current diff and suggest improvements to the newly added middleware'

steps.title: Sync and Version Control
steps.description: Finalize your work by committing the AI-validated code, ensuring a clean, documented history that clients can trust.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a concise, professional Git commit message that summarizes the implementation and the rationale behind the changes.
steps.claude_code_command: git commit -m 'feat(auth): implement JWT middleware with secure error handling'

pro_tips: Always include a .claudeprompt file in your root directory to enforce project-specific coding styles globally.
Use Git hooks to trigger Claude Code analysis automatically before every push to prevent CI/CD failures.
Treat your Git history as documentation; use AI to convert technical commits into client-facing release notes.
When working on complex bugs, ask the AI to generate a regression test suite before attempting the fix.
Keep your AI-generated code blocks small and modular to make it easier for Claude Code to maintain state without hallucinations.
resources: Git Best Practices for Freelancers: https://git-scm.com/book/en/v2
content.next_steps: Clone your next project, initialize a local Claude Code config, and commit to using this workflow for your next client feature request.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting AI overwhelm your process and start mastering the tools that turn code into profit. You have the blueprint, the tools, and the workflow. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
